meta.name: Chana Masala
meta.subtitle: Chickpea curry in a spiced onion-tomato masala

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/chana_masala.json
notes: Deterministic, weeknight-friendly version optimized for canned chickpeas.
Includes optional dried-chickpea + pressure-cooker variation.

summary: A classic North Indian chickpea curry: chickpeas simmered in a deeply flavored onion-tomato masala with warming spices, finished with lemon and cilantro.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a main with rice/roti; 6 as part of a larger spread.

notes: Naturally dairy-free if you do not add ghee or yogurt-based sides.

optional.label: Pressure cooker / Instant Pot
optional.notes: Only needed if using dried chickpeas variation.

ingredients.label: Chickpeas (canned), drained and rinsed
notes: Use 15 oz / 425 g cans.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
notes: Can substitute ghee (not vegan).

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder (or mild chili powder)
notes: Increase to 2 tsp for more heat.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
notes: Helps deepen color and flavor quickly.

ingredients.label: Crushed tomatoes
notes: Use 14–15 oz / ~400 g can.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Adjust for desired thickness.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Start with 1 tsp, adjust at the end.

ingredients.label: Amchur (dry mango powder)
notes: Or finish with extra lemon juice.

steps.title: Prep and rinse chickpeas
instructions: Drain and rinse the chickpeas well.
Chop onion; mince garlic; grate ginger; chop cilantro (if using).
checks: Rinsed chickpeas reduce canned taste and excess salt.

steps.title: Cook onions until golden
instructions: Heat oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add onion and cook 6–8 minutes, stirring often, until softened and lightly golden at the edges.
checks: Do not rush this: color here builds flavor.
If onions brown too fast, reduce heat to medium.

steps.title: Bloom aromatics and spices
instructions: Add garlic and ginger; cook 30–45 seconds until fragrant.
Add cumin, coriander, turmeric, and Kashmiri chili powder; stir 15–20 seconds to bloom.
If using tomato paste, stir it in for 30 seconds.
checks: Spices should smell toasted, not burnt.
If spices stick/darken fast, splash in 1–2 tbsp water to protect them.

steps.title: Cook the tomato masala
instructions: Simmer 8–10 minutes until the sauce darkens slightly and oil begins to separate at the edges.
checks: Masala is ready when it looks thicker and less raw.
If it splatters, lower heat and partially cover.

steps.title: Simmer chickpeas
instructions: Add chickpeas, water, salt (start with 1 tsp), and black pepper (optional).
Bring to a simmer and cook 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
For a thicker curry, mash a few spoonfuls of chickpeas against the pot wall and stir back in.
checks: Taste sauce: chickpeas should be infused and tender.
Adjust thickness by simmering uncovered (thicker) or adding water (thinner).

steps.title: Finish with garam masala and acid
instructions: Stir in garam masala and amchur (if using).
Taste and adjust salt. Stir in cilantro just before serving.
checks: Garam masala is best added near the end for aroma.
Acid at the end balances richness and sweetness from onions/tomatoes.

notes: Serve with rice or roti and a cooling side (cucumber, yogurt/raita if not vegan).
Flavor improves overnight.
Reheat gently with a splash of water if thickened.
Freeze in meal-size portions.
Thaw overnight and reheat on stovetop for best texture.
Cool quickly before refrigerating (shallow containers help).
Reheat to steaming hot throughout.
Soak 1 cup dried chickpeas overnight (or quick-soak).
Pressure cook with water and a pinch of salt until tender (Instant Pot ~35 min high pressure + natural release).
Use cooked chickpeas in the recipe; reduce added water slightly since you can use some chickpea cooking liquid.
Cook onions darker (deep golden) and simmer tomato masala longer.
Optional: add 1/2 tsp smoked paprika for deeper color (not traditional everywhere, but works).
Increase Kashmiri chili to 2 tsp or add 1/4–1/2 tsp cayenne.
Add sliced green chili with onions.
Excellent for batch cooking and meal prep.
Pairs well with rice or flatbread and a cooling side.
